title: Scope-safe language standard
note: Faculty should reinforce the distinction between triage support, patient education, and diagnosis at every stage of delivery.

title: Documentation quality threshold
note: Documentation tasks should be assessed against completeness, neutrality, and handover usefulness rather than sales style or personality.

title: Volume 1 Week 1 — faculty delivery
note: Source: IIOHR Consultant & Nurse Teaching Manual Volume 1 (Week 1). Encourage discussion of how commercial pressure can distort early patient communication. Compare patient journeys in high-trust versus high-pressure clinics. Use the manual practice activity: journey map from first enquiry to follow-up with five failure points and corrective actions.

title: Volume 1 Week 2 — faculty delivery
note: Source: Teaching Manual Volume 1 (Week 2: scope, ethics, professional responsibility). Use role-play: learners hear unsafe phrases aloud and rewrite them in calmer, bounded language. Classify phrases as within scope, borderline, or outside scope.

title: Volume 1 Week 3 — faculty delivery
note: Source: Teaching Manual Volume 1 (Week 3). Pair science with photographs or diagrams in live teaching. Emphasise translation into plain language, not jargon memorisation. Optional: collect a 300-word model answer for the thinning-despite-hairs exercise.

title: Volume 1 Week 4 — faculty delivery
note: Source: Teaching Manual Volume 1 (Week 4). Use ambiguous cases, not only obvious patterns. Normalise disciplined uncertainty and appropriate escalation. Six-case worksheet: pattern, red flags, routine vs urgent review, one patient-facing sentence for next step.

title: Capstone moderation note
note: Capstone review should confirm integrated use of scope, escalation, communication, and documentation principles before final award recommendation.
